input.query: Review current industry benchmarks for live web retrieval agents, compare vector ranking internals, and recommend a production deployment architecture with observability.

results.title: Comparing Review Architectures by Task Shape
Goal Contracts for Bounded Literature Review Assistants
PlanSketch: Lightweight Decomposition for Small Review Tasks
MemoryPolicy: When Notes Help and When They Mislead
Interface Contracts and Evidence Linking for Local Research Tools

output.title: Comparing Review Architectures by Task Shape
output.abstract: Architecture choice should be defended by observed outcomes on bounded tasks rather than by slogans such as 'agents are always better'. The paper explicitly contrasts model-only and scripted baselines before moving to richer agent variants.
findings: Task-architecture fit should be justified with observed outcomes and explicit failure diagnoses.

output.title: Goal Contracts for Bounded Literature Review Assistants
output.abstract: Structured goals, success criteria, and stop rules are treated as control inputs rather than prose decoration.
findings: Explicit success and stop criteria change downstream planning and evaluation behavior.

output.title: PlanSketch: Lightweight Decomposition for Small Review Tasks
output.abstract: Small literature reviews benefit from lightweight decomposition and selective replanning instead of exhaustive search trees.
findings: Lightweight decomposition adds enough structure for small review tasks without heavy formal planning.

output.title: MemoryPolicy: When Notes Help and When They Mislead
output.abstract: Stored notes improve continuity only when retrieval and forgetting policies are explicit. Stale notes create confident but brittle synthesis.
findings: Memory must include retrieval and forgetting policy, not just storage.

output.title: Interface Contracts and Evidence Linking for Local Research Tools
output.abstract: Research tools help only when tool outputs remain linked to the claims they support through explicit evidence-linking contracts.
findings: Tool interfaces change both capability and what evidence can ground the final review.
